title: crupina vulgaris subsp. morisii
intro: Crupina vulgaris subsp. morisii, also known as a thistle-like plant, is a subspecies of the Crupina vulgaris plant, a type of thistle native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Crupina vulgaris subsp. morisii include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, and are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures.
